messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
First, A and B approach each other at relative speed \(4\) cm/s, so they meet after

\[
\frac{40-10}{3-(-1)}=\frac{30}{4}=\frac{15}{2}\text{ s}.
\]

At that time, A and B are at \(32.5=\frac{65}{2}\) cm. After bouncing:
- A moves left at \(3\) cm/s,
- B moves right at \(1\) cm/s,
- C is at \(65\) cm moving left at \(2\) cm/s.

A reaches the 0 cm end after another

\[
\frac{65/2}{3}=\frac{65}{6}\text{ s}.
\]

So A falls off at

\[
\frac{15}{2}+\frac{65}{6}=\frac{55}{3}\text{ s}.
\]

At that same time, B and C meet at \(\frac{130}{3}\) cm. After bouncing:
- B moves left at \(1\) cm/s,
- C moves right at \(2\) cm/s.

B then takes

\[
\frac{130/3}{1}=\frac{130}{3}\text{ s}
\]

to fall off the 0 cm end, giving total time

\[
\frac{55}{3}+\frac{130}{3}=\frac{185}{3}.
\]

C falls off earlier, so the last ant is B.

ANSWER: 185/3
